title: Anchor forms include a final reminder group with Y/N flags for related forms
description: Forms that anchor a clinical visit may include a final group whose purpose is not data capture but operator reminder — Y/N flags asking whether other forms (AE, DV, DS) need to be completed for the same visit, paired with red-text reminder notes. The pattern is observed in customer authoring as a forms-completion safety net. DEFAULT BEHAVIOR: apply to 'anchor' forms (the longest or most encounter-defining form per visit, typically MH for baseline, PROC for procedure visit). Each safety-net group contains: one select_one YN row per related form ({FORM}AE_YN, {FORM}DV_YN, {FORM}DS_YN using IE-2a domain-prefix naming convention), plus one note row per Y/N, paired and gated per §22. UNIVERSAL SCOPE per §0 — applies to both customer-sourced and CDASH-default forms.
rationale: Common reactively-reported forms (AE, DV, DS) live on SE_COMMON per §7, which means coordinators must remember to navigate there separately when an event occurred during a visit. The forms-completion safety net brings the reminder into the anchor visit form: at the end of capturing MH or procedure data, the coordinator answers 'did any AE occur during this visit?' / 'any protocol deviations?' / 'any discontinuation?' If yes, the paired note (red-text per the gating in §22) prompts them to navigate to SE_COMMON. This reduces the silent-omission failure mode for reactive events.

flag.message: Form ${form.form_id} — if this is an 'anchor' form for its visit (longest or most encounter-defining; typically MH at baseline, PROC at procedure visit), append a final reminder group with Y/N rows ({FORM}AE_YN, {FORM}DV_YN, {FORM}DS_YN) and matching note rows gated per §22. Naming follows IE-2a domain-prefix convention. Surface in conventions_applied.forms_completion_safety_net.{applied_count, forms_with_safety_net}.

effect.soft: Anchor-form detection requires judgment: per visit, identify the longest or most encounter-defining form. For baseline visit, typically MH; for procedure visit, typically PROC. Generate the safety-net group with Y/N rows + paired notes; the §22 convention will then auto-gate the notes. Naming pattern: {FORM_ID}{RELATED_DOMAIN}_YN (e.g. MHAE_YN, MHDV_YN, MHDS_YN; PROCAE_YN, PROCDV_YN, PROCDS_YN). Customer-library overrides of the group structure are honored as-is.

history.note: Imported from conventions.md §20 during Phase B.1d. Hybrid because anchor-form detection requires per-visit form-length / form-purpose judgment that isn't a current DSL primitive. The safety-net group structure is well-defined (Y/N rows + paired notes per §22 gating); the trigger is the judgment-heavy part. Universal scope: customer-sourced anchor forms also benefit from the safety net pattern, and applying it doesn't conflict with existing customer authoring (the new group appends at the end without modifying authored content).
